surfaces.label: Mobile Cockpit (iPhone)
surfaces.notes: Near-perfect. Glassmorphic topbar, live chips, grid layout all correct.

surfaces.label: Mobile Answers (iPhone)
surfaces.notes: Clean list + expand affordance. Cascade conflict with cockpit.css resolved.

surfaces.label: Mobile Results (iPhone)
surfaces.notes: Data-only fail — HTTP 404 from local server (JSON feeds not served). CSS correct.

surfaces.label: Mobile Live (iPhone)
surfaces.notes: Pulse dot, Q/A inset, source tags all render correctly.

surfaces.label: Mobile Dispatch (iPhone)
surfaces.notes: Data-only fail — same server issue. CSS structure intact.

surfaces.label: Mobile Cockpit (iPad Pro)
surfaces.notes: Perfect score. Two-column layout, proportional scaling, full token compliance.

surfaces.label: Mobile Answers (iPad Pro)
surfaces.notes: Two-column grid renders correctly at 1024px breakpoint.

surfaces.label: Mobile Results (iPad Pro)
surfaces.notes: Data-only fail — same server issue.

surfaces.label: Mobile Live (iPad Pro)
surfaces.notes: Two-column card grid, pulse animation, proper dark token usage.

surfaces.label: Desktop Dashboard
surfaces.notes: Solid. Well-structured sections, consistent iconography.

surfaces.label: RSN Deals
surfaces.notes: FIXED 2026-04-28: converted from light mode (#f6f7f9 bg) to full EverPass dark system. Was 14/30 FAIL.

surfaces.label: Content Pipeline Flow
surfaces.notes: Perfect score. Best-in-class dark diagram layout.

surfaces.label: RSN Decision Framing
surfaces.notes: Initially scored 13 from compressed screenshot; re-examined CSS and rescored 23.

surfaces.label: TWDC/ESPN Negotiation
surfaces.notes: Light-mode remnants, low contrast section headers.

surfaces.notes: Borderline. Low visual hierarchy on data fields.

surfaces.notes: Minimal pass. Acceptable density, adequate contrast.

summary.fix_detail: RSN Deals: light-mode → dark-mode conversion (+12 pts, FAIL→PASS)
summary.note: 3 mobile FAIL scores (Results iPhone, Dispatch iPhone, Results iPad) are data-server failures not CSS failures — CSS is correct but local dev server doesn't serve JSON feeds at the expected path. Design is PASS-quality.
